powershell (83-117)
PS C:\WINDOWS\system32> curl.exe http://[IP_ADDRESS]:3030/health
curl: (7) Failed to connect to [IP_ADDRESS] port 3030 after 2032 ms: Could not connect to server
PS C:\WINDOWS\system32> SCREENPIPE_URL=http://[IP_ADDRESS]:<that-port>
SCREENPIPE_URL=http://[IP_ADDRESS]:<that-port> : The term 'SCREENPIPE_URL=http://[IP_ADDRESS]:<that-port>' is not recognized
as the name of a cmdlet, function, script file, or operable program. Check the spelling of the name, or if a path was
included, verify that the path is correct and try again.
At line:1 char:1
+ SCREENPIPE_URL=http://[IP_ADDRESS]:<that-port>
+ ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
+ CategoryInfo          : ObjectNotFound: (SCREENPIPE_URL=...0.1:<that-port>:String) [], CommandNotFoundException
+ FullyQualifiedErrorId : CommandNotFoundException
PS C:\WINDOWS\system32> screenpipe
screenpipe : The term 'screenpipe' is not recognized as the name of a cmdlet, function, script file, or operable
program. Check the spelling of the name, or if a path was included, verify that the path is correct and try again.
At line:1 char:1
+ screenpipe
+ ~~~~~~~~~~
+ CategoryInfo          : ObjectNotFound: (screenpipe:String) [], CommandNotFoundException
+ FullyQualifiedErrorId : CommandNotFoundException
PS C:\WINDOWS\system32>
Here’s what’s going on and how to fix it.
1. curl failing
Nothing is accepting connections on
[IP_ADDRESS]:3030
, so
Screenpipe’s API isn’t running
(or it’s on another port). Setting
SCREENPIPE_URL
only helps
after
something is actually listening; it doesn’t start Screenpipe.
Do this first:
install and
open the Screenpipe app
(or start the server you use). Official site:
https://screenpi.pe
— use the Windows build from their docs/releases. After it’s running, try:
curl.exe
http:
//
127.0
.
0.1
:
3030
/
health
If that still fails, check the app’s
Settings
for
API port
(it might not be 3030).
2. Why SCREENPIPE_URL=... failed (PowerShell vs bash)
That line is
bash/Linux
syntax. In
PowerShell
use:
$
env:SCREENPIPE_URL
=
"
http://[IP_ADDRESS]:3030
"
Replace
3030
with the real port from Screenpipe if it’s different.
That only lasts for
this
terminal session. For your Node pipeline, it’s easier to put it in the project
.env
file (same folder as
package.json
):
SCREENPIPE_URL=http://[IP_ADDRESS]:3030
(Again, change the port if the app says otherwise.)
3. Why screenpipe isn’t a command
Normal on Windows
unless you’ve installed a CLI or added a built
screenpipe.exe
to your PATH. You don’t need the
screenpipe
command in PowerShell if you use the
desktop app
— just
launch the app
from the Start menu.
If you want a
from-source
build, that’s what
scripts/setup-screenpipe.ps1
/
vendor/
screenpipe
in your repo is for; that’s heavier than installing the official app.
Order of operations
Install + start Screenpipe (GUI).
Confirm curl.exe http://[IP_ADDRESS]:3030/health (or whatever port the app shows).
curl.exe http://[IP_ADDRESS]:3030/health
Put that base URL in .env as SCREENPIPE_URL if it’s not 3030.
.env
SCREENPIPE_URL
From your project folder, run npm run e2e:screenpipe -- --minutes 5 again.
npm run e2e:screenpipe -- --minutes 5

Thought briefly
Use this as a
copy-paste prompt
for Claude:
Prompt for Claude
You are helping run an end-to-end test for the
openclaw-video-skill-pipeline
repository on
Windows
.
Goal:
Run the full pipeline:
Screenpipe (last N minutes of capture) → Gemini SKILL.md → MiniMax SOP compile → export under
skills/export/<slug>/
, then confirm outputs exist.
Hard prerequisites (verify before running Node):
Screenpipe is installed and running (desktop app or local server with HTTP API).
From PowerShell, curl.exe http://[IP_ADDRESS]:3030/health returns JSON (not connection refused). If Screenpipe uses a different port, note it and set SCREENPIPE_URL in the repo .env to http://[IP_ADDRESS]:<port> (PowerShell session env: $env:SCREENPIPE_URL = "http://[IP_ADDRESS]:<port>").
curl.exe http://[IP_ADDRESS]:3030/health
SCREENPIPE_URL
.env
http://[IP_ADDRESS]:<port>
$env:SCREENPIPE_URL = "http://[IP_ADDRESS]:<port>"
Repo root: openclaw-video-skill-pipeline with .env containing at least GEMINI_API_KEY and MINIMAX_API_KEY (for full E2E including compile). To skip MiniMax, use --no-compile.
openclaw-video-skill-pipeline
.env
GEMINI_API_KEY
MINIMAX_API_KEY
--no-compile
There must be recent Screenpipe data in the chosen time window (e.g. user activity in the last 5 minutes), or widen --minutes.
--minutes
Steps to execute:
cd to the repo root.
cd
Ensure dependencies: npm install at repo root (use npm install --ignore-scripts if screenpipe postinstall fails on Windows). If MCP subpackage is needed separately, cd mcp/badass-skills-server && npm install — not required for E2E unless testing MCP.
npm install
npm install --ignore-scripts
screenpipe
cd mcp/badass-skills-server && npm install
Run:
npm run e2e:screenpipe -- --minutes 5
(or npm run e2e:screenpipe -- --minutes 5 --no-compile to skip MiniMax.)
npm run e2e:screenpipe -- --minutes 5
npm run e2e:screenpipe -- --minutes 5 --no-compile
On success, report:• Path to skills/export/<slug>/SKILL.md• Paths under skills/export/<slug>/artifacts/ (screenpipe-search.json, and minimax-compilation.json if compile ran)• The pipeline-output/screenpipe-live_* folder used
Path to skills/export/<slug>/SKILL.md
skills/export/<slug>/SKILL.md
Paths under skills/export/<slug>/artifacts/ (screenpipe-search.json, and minimax-compilation.json if compile ran)
skills/export/<slug>/artifacts/
screenpipe-search.json
minimax-compilation.json
The pipeline-output/screenpipe-live_* folder used
pipeline-output/screenpipe-live_*
If it fails, capture the first error (e.g. ECONNREFUSED → Screenpipe not running or wrong port; empty OCR → no data in time window).
Optional follow-up (only if the user wants publishing):
After a successful export, explain that
skills/export/<slug>
must be
committed and pushed
, then GitHub Actions
“Publish skill to badass-skills”
must be run with
source_path
=
skills/export/<slug>
and matching
skill_slug
, with
BADASS_SKILLS_PUBLISH_TOKEN
set in the repo.
Do not
print or commit API keys or tokens. Summarize pass/fail and file paths only.
You can shorten the “Optional follow-up” block if you only care about local E2E, not publishing.
